title: Pre-Monsoon Rains Disrupt North Sikkim Roads; Mangan–Chungthang Route Restored - Northeast Live
article_text:
Pre-Monsoon Rains Disrupt North Sikkim Roads; Mangan–Chungthang Route Restored

First Published: 24th May, 2026 12:37 IST

The crucial Meyong Khola bridge has been reopened for the movement of all types of vehicles.

Written By Northeast Live Digital Desk SHARE IT NOW







The key road connecting Mangan and Chungthang via Toong Naga has now been reopened for tourists after being temporarily disrupted on Saturday due to a sudden rise in the water level of the Meyong Khola River amid pre-monsoon rains in Sikkim.

An official notification issued by the office of the SP, Mangan, stated that the crucial Meyong Khola bridge has been reopened for the movement of all types of vehicles.

As a precautionary measure, concerned authorities earlier diverted all vehicles back to their respective locations, while advising all travellers to stay alert and remain in safe places until situation normalises. The vehicular movement on both sides of the road was halted as a precautionary measure, as per reports.

Around 45 tourists stranded in the area were accommodated at the Chungthang Gurudwara, where they were provided free night shelter and meals.

Meanwhile, the road connecting Chungthang to Lachen in North Sikkim has remained blocked for the past six weeks due to adverse weather conditions, while restoration work is currently underway. Mangan Superintendent of Police (SP) LB Chettri, accompanied by the Sub-Divisional Police Officer (SDPO) of Chungthang and the Station House Officer (SHO) of Lachen, on Friday inspected the ongoing road restoration work at Taram Chu.

Post Views: 28

COMMENTS

title: Andhra, Telangana Weather Today: Scorching Heat With No Relief Until Monsoon Arrives - The Morning Voice
article_text:
Andhra, Telangana Weather Today: Scorching Heat With No Relief Until Monsoon Arrives
Yekkirala Akshitha
May 24, 2026

The IMD forecasts dry weather very likely across Telangana on May 24 , no rain, no cloud cover to speak of, and no warning issued. Hyderabad wakes to a sunrise at 5:42 AM and faces a high of around 38°C (101°F) , a low of 28°C, with 31% humidity , unusually dry even for May northwesterly winds of 10–15 mph, a mere 4% chance of rain , and an extreme UV index from first light. Sunset is at 6:43 PM. The hours in between are best spent indoors.

Districts under active IMD heatwave alerts include Adilabad, Mancherial, Peddapalli, Karimnagar, Jagtial, Nizamabad, Nirmal, Suryapet, and Nalgonda, a continuous band across Telangana's north and centre. The state's highest temperature this summer was recorded at 46.5°C in Dilawarpur, Nirmal , with Dharmasagar, Choppadandi, and Odela all reaching 46.4°C .

The human cost is no longer abstract. Telangana's Revenue Minister confirmed 16 heatwave deaths across seven districts , announcing ₹4 lakh ex-gratia to each victim's family and ordering an emergency review of precautionary measures. The actual toll is likely higher, official government data recorded just 10 heatwave deaths in 2024 while NCRB police data counted 116 in the same year, a gap of 106 lives that reflects how systematically India undercounts heat mortality. India's peak electricity demand hit a record 256 gigawatts in April 2026 , driven overwhelmingly by air conditioning and cooling loads and Telangana's grid is under similar strain, with power demand rising sharply alongside temperatures in recent weeks.

In Nandyal , deep in Andhra Pradesh's Rayalaseema, the heat is structurally worse. Temperatures are expected to hover between 43°C and 44°C , baked further by the town's position in the rain shadow of the Eastern Ghats , which blocks moisture-laden winds from the Bay of Bengal. The dry northwesterly hot winds sweeping in from the Thar Desert compound the misery. No relief is forecast until May 28 for Andhra Pradesh. For farmers eyeing the approaching Kharif sowing season, every additional day of this heat depletes already stressed soil moisture and groundwater levels, particularly acute in Rayalaseema, which is chronically water-scarce.

Authorities have strongly advised residents not to step outside after 10 AM unless absolutely necessary , to stay hydrated with water, coconut water, and ORS, and to take special care of children, the elderly, and outdoor workers. School timing advisories and construction worker safety orders have been circulated across both states.

The monsoon offers the only exit. The southwest monsoon is expected to reach Kerala on May 26 , with Telangana likely to see its first rains by early June . The cruel irony of the pre-monsoon week is that as atmospheric instability builds ahead of the seasonal shift, temperatures often intensify before they break, making the final days of May the most punishing of the entire summer.

title: Tamil Nadu weather today: வெயிலுக்கு ரெஸ்ட்.. 19 மாவட்டத்தில் இன்று வெளுக்கப்போகுது கனமழை.. வானிலை எச்சரிக்கை - Times Now Tamil
article_text:
Tamil Nadu weather today: தமிழகத்தில் கடந்த 24 மணி நேரத்தில் நெல்லை, கடலூர், கள்ளக்குறிச்சி, திருப்பத்தூர் ஆகிய மாவட்டங்களில் மழை பெய்துள்ளது. அதிகபட்சமாக நெல்லை மாவட்டம் நாலுமுக்கு பகுதியில் அதிகபட்சமாக 40 மி.மீ மழை பதிவாகியுள்ளது. அதேவேளையில் தமிழகத்தில் ஓரிரு இடங்களில் வெப்பநிலை இயல்வை விட 2 டிகிரி செல்சியஸ் குறைந்துள்ளது. கொடைக்கானல், ஊட்டியில் இதமான காலநிலை நிலவி வருகிறது. ராமநாதபுரத்தில் நேற்று வெயிலின் தாக்கம் அதிகமாக இருந்தது. இந்நிலையில் வளிமண்டல சுழற்சி காரணமாக தமிழகத்தில் அடுத்த சில தினங்களுக்கு மழைக்கு வாய்ப்பு இருப்பதாக சென்னை வானிலை ஆய்வு மையம் தெரிவித்துள்ளது.

இதுதொடர்பான வானிலை மையம் வெளியிட்டுள்ள செய்திக்குறிப்பில், “ தமிழகத்தில் இன்று நீலகிரி, கோயம்புத்தூர், ஈரோடு, திருப்பூர், சேலம், தர்மபுரி, கிருஷ்ணகிரி, திருப்பத்தூர், வேலூர், ராணிப்பேட்டை, திருவண்ணாமலை, கள்ளக்குறிச்சி, பெரம்பலூர், திருச்சிராப்பள்ளி, நாமக்கல், கரூர், திண்டுக்கல், தேனி மற்றும் மதுரை மாவட்டங்களில் ஓரிரு இடங்களில் இடி மின்னல் மற்றும் பலத்த காற்றுடன் (மணிக்கு 40 முதல் 50 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்தில்) கூடிய கன மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.தமிழகத்தில் ஒருசில இடங்களிலும், புதுவை மற்றும் காரைக்கால் பகுதிகளிலும் இடி மின்னலுடன் கூடிய லேசானது முதல் மிதமான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.

இதையும் படிங்க: புதுச்சேரியில் நாளை மின் தடை அறிவிப்பு.. 4 மணி நேரம் மின் வினியோகம் இருக்காது

25.05.2026:நீலகிரி, கோயம்புத்தூர், ஈரோடு, திருப்பூர், சேலம், தர்மபுரி, கிருஷ்ணகிரி, திருப்பத்தூர், வேலூர், ராணிப்பேட்டை, திருவண்ணாமலை, கள்ளக்குறிச்சி, பெரம்பலூர், திருச்சிராப்பள்ளி, நாமக்கல், கரூர், திண்டுக்கல், தேனி மற்றும் மதுரை மாவட்டங்களில் ஓரிரு இடங்களில் இடி மின்னல் மற்றும் பலத்த காற்றுடன் (மணிக்கு 40 முதல் 50 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்தில்) கூடிய கன மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.தமிழகத்தில் ஒருசில இடங்களிலும், புதுவை மற்றும் காரைக்கால் பகுதிகளிலும் இடி மின்னலுடன் கூடிய லேசானது முதல் மிதமான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.

26-05-2026: கோயம்புத்தூர் மற்றும் திருப்பூர் மாவட்டங்களின் மலைப்பகுதிகள், நீலகிரி ,தேனி, திண்டுக்கல், விருதுநகர், தென்காசி திருநெல்வேலி மற்றும் கன்னியாகுமரி மாவட்டங்களில் ஓரிரு இடங்களில் இடி மின்னல் மற்றும் பலத்த காற்றுடன் (மணிக்கு 40 முதல் 50 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்தில்) கூடிய கன மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.தமிழகத்தில் ஓரிரு இடங்களிலும், புதுவை மற்றும் காரைக்கால் பகுதிகளிலும் இடி மின்னலுடன் கூடிய லேசானது முதல் மிதமான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.

இதையும் படிங்க: கோவை மாவட்ட மகளிர் அதிகார மையத்தில் வேலை.. மாதம் ரூ.21,000 சம்பளம்.. முழு விவரம் இதோ

27-05-2026 முதல் 29-05-2026 வரை: தமிழகத்தில் ஓரிரு இடங்களிலும், புதுவை மற்றும் காரைக்கால் பகுதிகளிலும் லேசானது முதல் மிதமான பெய்யக்கூடும்.

சென்னை வானிலை நிலவரம்:

சென்னையில் இன்று வானம் ஓரளவு மேகமூட்டத்துடன் காணப்படும். மாலை / இரவு நேரங்களில் இடி மின்னலுடன் கூடிய லேசானது முதல் மிதமான மழை பெய்ய வாய்ப்புள்ளது. அதிகபட்ச வெப்பநிலை 39-40° செல்சியஸை ஒட்டியும், குறைந்தபட்ச வெப்பநிலை 29-30° செல்சியஸை ஒட்டியும் இருக்கக்கூடும்.

மீனவர்களுக்கான எச்சரிக்கை:

தமிழக கடலோரப்பகுதிகள்:

தமிழக கடலோரப்பகுதிகள், மன்னார் வளைகுடா மற்றும் குமரிக்கடல் பகுதிகளில் சூறாவளிக்காற்று மணிக்கு 40 முதல் 50 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்திலும் இடையிடையே 60 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்திலும் வீசக்கூடும்.

வங்கக்கடல் பகுதிகள்:

தென்மேற்கு வங்கக்கடலின் சில பகுதிகளில் சூறாவளிக்காற்று மணிக்கு 45 முதல் 55 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்திலும் இடையிடையே 65 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்திலும், தெற்கு வங்கக்கடலின் அநேக பகுதிகளில், தென் ஆந்திரப் பிரதேச கடலோரம் மற்றும் அதனை ஒட்டிய வடஆந்திரப் பிரதேச கடலோர பகுதிகளில் சூறாவளிக்காற்று மணிக்கு 40 முதல் 50 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்திலும் இடையிடையே 60 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்திலும் வீசக்கூடும்.” எனத் தெரிவிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது.

title: Karnataka Weather Turns Monsoon-Like IMD Issues 7-Day Heavy Rain Forecast & Hailstorm Warning - Goodreturns
article_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) and Karnataka State Natural Disaster Monitoring Centre (KSNDMC) have issued a fresh Karnataka weather forecast and rain warning for the next seven days, predicting widespread rainfall, thunderstorms, lightning, gusty winds, and isolated heavy rain across several districts of the state.
According to the latest Karnataka weather update, light to moderate rain with thunderstorms and strong surface winds is expected across many parts of Karnataka today and tomorrow. The IMD has also warned that hailstorm activity may occur in isolated interior districts of Karnataka on May 25.
The latest weather warning comes as several districts continue to record intense rainfall activity, with some areas receiving more than 150 mm rainfall in the last 24 hours.
As per the latest IMD Bengaluru weather bulletin, there is a strong indication of:
The Karnataka weather department has advised residents to remain cautious during thunderstorm activity, especially in districts vulnerable to strong winds and lightning strikes.
The 7-day Karnataka rain forecast issued by IMD shows continued rainfall activity across Coastal Karnataka, North Interior Karnataka, and South Interior Karnataka.
IMD has predicted thunderstorms and lightning with squally weather conditions from May 24 to May 27 in coastal districts. No major warning has been issued from May 28 onwards.
North Interior Karnataka districts are expected to witness thunderstorms, lightning, and squally winds between May 24 and May 27. Hailstorm activity is likely on May 25 in isolated places.
South Interior Karnataka, including Bengaluru and nearby districts, is also likely to receive moderate rainfall accompanied by thunder and lightning till May 27. A hailstorm warning has also been issued for some interior areas on May 25.
Several districts witnessed extremely heavy rainfall during the last 24 hours. According to KSNDMC rainfall data recorded between 8:30 AM on May 23 and 7:30 AM on May 24, the highest rainfall was reported from:
The IMD Bengaluru nowcast bulletin has specifically warned districts including Ballari, Koppal, and Dharwad about moderate rain and thundershowers accompanied by gusty winds reaching up to 40-50 kmph over the next few hours.
The weather office warned that the ongoing rain activity could lead to Temporary disruption of electricity, Traffic congestion, Damage to weak structures, Falling tree branches & Crop damage in open agricultural lands
Residents have been advised to stay indoors during lightning activity, avoid taking shelter under trees, and unplug electrical appliances during thunderstorms.
Weather experts say the current rainfall activity has been triggered due to strong moisture incursion and atmospheric instability over the state. The rain intensity is expected to remain high over interior and coastal Karnataka till May 27 before gradually reducing in some regions.
However, isolated showers and thunderstorms may continue in several districts even after the warning period.

title: Monsoon 2026: Here's IMD's latest prediction on arrival dates for Kerala to Delhi - MSN
article_text:
As large parts of India continue to reel under intense summer heat, attention is now shifting to the arrival of the southwest monsoon — the weather system that delivers nearly 70% of the country’s annual rainfall and plays a crucial role in agriculture, water supply and power demand.
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the southwest monsoon is expected to reach the Kerala coast around its normal onset date in early June, even as some global weather models hint at a slightly early arrival this year.
Must read | From food prices to power supply: Why monsoon still decides the fate of India’s economy
IMD Director General Mrutyunjay Mohapatra said monsoon rains are likely to begin over the Andaman and Nicobar Islands between May 14 and May 20 — an important early milestone in the monsoon’s journey toward mainland India.
What is the usual monsoon timeline?
Under normal conditions, the southwest monsoon follows a fairly predictable progression across the country:
Eggs are a versatile and nutrient-rich staple found in kitchens worldwide, valued for their protein, vitamins, and culinary flexibility. From breakfast omelets to baked goods, they are a key ingredient in countless recipes. Despite their benefits, eggs can sometimes harbour harmful bacteria if they are past their prime, improperly stored, or damaged. Consuming spoiled eggs can lead to foodborne illnesses, making it crucial to recognise early warning signs. By learning to identify changes in appearance, texture, smell, and freshness, you can ensure your eggs are safe to eat and protect your health with every meal.
The first indicator of egg safety is the label on the carton. Eggs usually feature a sell-by date, expiration date, or pack date. The sell-by date indicates when a store should sell eggs for peak quality, while the expiration date marks the last day the manufacturer recommends consuming them. Pack dates reveal when eggs were processed. According to the US Food and Drug Administration eggs are typically safe for four to five weeks after the pack date if stored properly. While dates provide guidance, combining them with other freshness tests ensures you avoid consuming eggs that may be unsafe.
A cracked or broken shell compromises the egg’s natural protective barrier, allowing bacteria to enter. Even small cracks can affect freshness and safety. When inspecting eggs, check all sides and gently run your fingers over the surface. If you find any damage, it’s best to discard the egg rather than risk contamination, as cooking may not eliminate all harmful bacteria.
A slimy texture on the shell can indicate bacterial contamination. Eggs with this coating should be discarded immediately, and hands should be washed thoroughly after handling. Harmful bacteria such as Salmonella can be present on the shell and inside the egg, causing gastrointestinal illness and, in severe cases, more serious complications in vulnerable individuals. Refrigeration at or below 40°F (4°C) helps prevent bacterial growth and prolongs freshness.
A powdery or fuzzy coating may indicate mold or fungal contamination. Mold growth often occurs when eggs are stored in warm, humid, or unhygienic conditions. Common molds include Penicillium, Alternaria, and Rhizopus, which can produce toxins harmful if ingested. Mold may also penetrate the egg, creating black spots inside. Eggs showing these signs should be discarded immediately to prevent illness.
The water test is a simple method to check freshness. Place eggs in a bowl of water: fresh eggs sink and lie flat, while older eggs may tilt or float due to gas buildup inside. Floating eggs are not automatically unsafe but should be cracked open to check for unpleasant odors or visible spoilage before consumption.
Smell is a strong indicator of egg safety. Fresh eggs have a neutral odor, while spoiled eggs release hydrogen sulfide, producing a sulfur-like smell. If an egg emits a foul odor, discard it immediately. Cracking the egg into a bowl allows you to assess the smell more accurately. Eating eggs with an offensive odor can lead to food poisoning.
Egg whites should be clear or slightly translucent. Green or pink discoloration often indicates bacterial spoilage, such as from Pseudomonas, which can cause foodborne illness. Note that blood spots in egg whites are harmless and not a sign of spoilage. Overcooked eggs may also develop a green ring around the yolk, which is safe to eat.
Fresh egg whites are thick and viscous. If whites are runny or watery, it can signal age or improper storage. Egg producers measure freshness using Haugh units, with higher values indicating fresher eggs. While not all runny whites are unsafe, pronounced liquidity, especially alongside other warning signs, warrants discarding the egg.
A fresh egg yolk is rounded and firm, with a vibrant yellow or orange hue. Flattened, sunken, or discolored yolks may indicate poor quality or aging. Discoloration can result from storage conditions, hen diet, or medication. While slight mottling is common, severe flattening or unusual coloring is a strong signal to avoid consumption.
Shaking an egg can reveal internal changes. Fresh eggs produce little sound, while older eggs may slosh due to gas expansion in the air cell. This test alone isn’t foolproof, but combined with visual and olfactory checks, it can help identify eggs past their prime.
Candling involves shining a light through an egg to check the size of the air cell. Fresh eggs have a small air cell, while older eggs display a larger bubble. Although a larger air cell alone doesn’t make the egg unsafe, it’s a sign of aging and should be assessed with other freshness indicators.
Eggs are nutritious and versatile, but it’s essential to monitor for signs of spoilage. By checking expiry dates, shell integrity, texture, color, odor, and using simple tests like floating, shaking, or candling, you can ensure the eggs you consume are safe. Proper storage and handling further reduce the risk of foodborne illness, keeping your meals both delicious and healthy. Incorporating these safety checks into your routine builds awareness, prevents accidental consumption of contaminated eggs, and empowers households to maintain better food hygiene practices for overall health and wellbeing.
Disclaimer: This article is for general informational purposes only and is not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always seek the guidance of a qualified healthcare provider regarding any medical condition or lifestyle change.
